demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; spouse present and assisting

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My thighs and lower back feel like they are being crushed in a vise, the pain is ten out of ten, and my normal strong painkillers are doing absolutely nothing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I have sickle cell disease and I've had one of these crushing pain attacks come on over the last few hours. I've taken all my usual tablets and they haven't touched it, and I can't cope with the pain.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 6 hours ago, and it got much worse quickly after I got a bit cold earlier today
symptom_narrative.character: deep, crushing, boring pain in the bones — like my thigh bones and lower back are being squeezed in a vice
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: ten out of ten, the worst kind of crisis pain I get
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both thighs and my lower back
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, I can't get comfortable in any position
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — my usual strong painkillers have done absolutely nothing
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: getting cold seemed to set it off and moving makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel wiped out and drained, more exhausted than my usual tiredness, and a bit sick in the stomach. My eyes and skin have gone yellow like they sometimes do.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get these pain crises from my sickle cell disease and I've needed hospital and transfusions before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't get out of bed, I'm just writhing around trying to find a position that helps

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: severe sickle cell disease, needing regular blood transfusions
past_medical_history.how_managed: haematology team, regular transfusions, strong painkillers at home for crises

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I've had my spleen taken out
a sore on my ankle that won't heal
past_medical_history.how_managed: ongoing wound on the outer right ankle

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing anaemia and tiredness from the sickle cell
past_medical_history.how_managed: part of the sickle cell disease

family_history_as_reported.narrative: sickle cell runs in the family

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is present and helping; wrapped up warm in blankets, very distressed with the pain

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of the eyes and skin look yellow (jaundiced); looks very pale in the lips and inner eyelids; sweaty and grimacing, shifting constantly in bed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient/carer visual impression over video — colour and pallor are hard to judge remotely and lighting-dependent
single reading on an uncalibrated home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: spouse reports she is writhing and crying out with the pain, wrapped in thick blankets, feels clammy, and has empty painkiller blister packs by the bed from tonight
a roughly 2 cm open sore on the outer right ankle that hasn't healed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-shown wound over video, not clinician-examined

objective_data_offered.value: took the maximum daily dose of oxycodone and paracetamol over the last 6 hours with no relief at all
